response.justification: Candidate is a solid senior software craftsman with broad full-stack experience and some recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, but the role demands a CTO-level executive with 5+ years senior technical leadership, proven production multi-agent AI systems at scale, fintech-infrastructure depth, and a track record scaling teams from 10 to 30+. The resume shows individual-contributor roles (Senior/Staff Software Developer), no evidence of leading engineering organizations, no production multi-agent AI systems, and only tangential fintech exposure via RIDE Capital. The gap between the candidate's profile and an executive CTO mandate is too large to justify an interview.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background across web, mobile, embedded, and distributed systems
Recent hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq
Prior high-throughput distributed-systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of req/sec
concerns: No executive or engineering-leadership roles — entire career is IC titles, no evidence of scaling teams 10→30+
No demonstrated production multi-agent AI systems; agent work appears recent and tooling-focused, not architectural at scale
Minimal fintech-infrastructure depth — only ~15 months at RIDE Capital with no credit/risk/working-capital systems mentioned

key_factors.factor: Lack of executive leadership track record
No production multi-agent AI systems experience
Some recent agentic AI / MCP exposure
